name: ExtensionDosage_MaxDosePerPeriod
title: R5: maxDosePerPeriod
purpose:
This extension is part of the cross-version definitions generated to enable use of the
element `Dosage.maxDosePerPeriod` as defined in FHIR R5
in FHIR R4.

The source element is defined as:
`Dosage.maxDosePerPeriod` 0..* `Ratio`

Across FHIR versions, the element set has been mapped as:
*  R5: `Dosage.maxDosePerPeriod` 0..* `Ratio`
*  R4B: `Dosage.maxDosePerPeriod` 0..1 `Ratio`
*  R4: `Dosage.maxDosePerPeriod` 0..1 `Ratio`

Following are the generation technical comments:
Element `Dosage.maxDosePerPeriod` allows multiple values in the source, but is mapped to an element that does not allow multiple values in the target.
Element `Dosage.maxDosePerPeriod` is mapped to FHIR R4 element `Dosage.maxDosePerPeriod` as `SourceIsBroaderThanTarget`.

element.comment:
Element `Dosage.maxDosePerPeriod` allows multiple values in the source, but is mapped to an element that does not allow multiple values in the target.
Element `Dosage.maxDosePerPeriod` is mapped to FHIR R4 element `Dosage.maxDosePerPeriod` as `SourceIsBroaderThanTarget`.
This is intended for use as an adjunct to the dosage when there is an upper cap.  For example "2 tablets every 4 hours to a maximum of 8/day".

element.short: Upper limit on medication per unit of time
element.definition: Upper limit on medication per unit of time.
